subject: [N5] 35 action items from 2025-09-24 external-alex-wisdom-partners-coaching
body:
Hi V,

I extracted 35 action items from your 2025-09-24 external-alex-wisdom-partners-coaching meeting (20251028-132902_Meetings).
Please review and reply with your approval:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

**1. # DETAILED_RECAP**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 meeting
   📝 From meeting recap...

**2. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**3. Feedback**: - [ ] Useful**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**4. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**5. ## Key Decisions and Agreements**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**6. **Pivot employer outreach to "candidate cards" approach** — Alex recommended creating digestible "playing card" style candidate summaries (picture, 2-3 experience points, 2-3 story snippets) delivered via trackable URL bundles. This breaks the mold of resume-heavy recruiter outreach and provides instant value to hiring managers.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**7. **Target hiring managers directly, not HR/talent teams** — Rationale: HR likes traditional resume review processes (job security), but busy team leads/engineering managers will respond better to human-centric, pre-filtered candidate presentations. Hiring managers have authority to skip ATS anyway.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**8. **Implement "goods up front" sales approach** — Send candidate bundles immediately without building relationship first. Alex noted recruiters waste time trying to establish rapport before showing candidates, which is annoying. Lead with value: "Here are candidates, do you want to pay for them?"**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**9. **Use URL hosting for engagement metrics** — Track click-through rates, profiles browsed, time spent to optimize outreach strategy. Each URL is unique per employer to gather performance data.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**10. **Categorize candidates thematically, not percentage fit scores** — Sections like "Clear Fits" (on-paper matches) and "Hidden Gems" (career transitioners, atypical profiles) feel more curated and human than algorithmic percentage scores.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**11. ## Strategic Context**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**12. Product Positioning**: Careerspan has shifted from selling behavioral insights (too high burden of proof, buyers jaded) to a staffing play: "If we get good candidates in and introduce them to employers, we make money." This is more objective and testable.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**13. Current GTM**: Targeting product management roles in NYC/SF/Boston. Using "magic link" system (candidates apply through Careerspan), embedding in communities, reaching out to high-quality candidates directly. Employers agreed to $300-400 per warm intro, contingent on seeing quality.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**14. Product Strength**: Alex praised the coaching interface simplicity and story accumulation mechanic (candidates tell stories to fill gaps, stories persist across applications). Noted this creates defensible candidate data that traditional recruiters can't replicate. The nuanced chatbot implementation (speeds up/slows down based on response quality) impressed technical users.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**15. Hiring Manager Pain Point**: Resume review is "mind-numbingly boring" and time-consuming. Product managers especially have varied backgrounds (career transitioners, IC-to-PM pivots), making atypical profiles harder to evaluate. Careerspan's story-based insights surface "what the resume misses."**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**16. Pitch Event Context**: Vrijen preparing for Atlanta pitch event ($2k entry, founder referral). Alex advised pitch events are typically low-value for customers but excellent for founder practice (confidence projection, timing, conciseness). Vrijen acknowledged need to be "front-footed" after previous insecurity about product quality—now has data showing strong engagement metrics.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**17. ## Critical Next Action**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**18. Owner**: Vrijen + Logan**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**19. Deliverable**: Prototype "candidate card" URL system with employer-facing bundle**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**20. Timeline**: ASAP (test before scaling outreach)**
   📅 Today 3:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🔴 High
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**21. Purpose**: Validate whether "goods up front" + playing card UX converts hiring managers better than traditional recruiter outreach. This unlocks all GTM channels (community partnerships, cold outreach, subscription model).**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**22. **Candidate card system**: Logan and I are prototyping the "playing card" style bundles (experience + stories, trackable URLs) as the core employer deliverable**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...

**23. **Hiring manager targeting**: Shifting outreach away from HR/talent teams toward team leads who prioritize efficiency over process**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...

**24. **Thematic categorization**: Testing "Clear Fits" and "Hidden Gems" groupings instead of percentage scores to feel more curated**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...

**25. **Job posting age segmentation**: Building pipeline to test fresh vs. stale posting timing for outreach**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...

**26. I'll update you once we have the candidate card prototype live—would love your gut check on whether it hits the "digestible and enticing" bar you described**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...

**27. Atlanta pitch is tomorrow; I'll report back in ~2 weeks on whether the $2k experiment panned out (fingers crossed for some practice reps either way)**
   📅 Tomorrow 10:00am | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...
